title: church
body:
verse 1

my church is kendrick
his church is compton
that's the difference
between the words we offer
that's not deference
i acknowledge the altar
that i burn my sage on
before i get my sage on
that's a kindred spirit
i'm proud to pull a page from
my ink, my paper
his words and work
gave seed to my labor
i think, i drink, and i savor
then report my perspective
on how the day was

verse 2

but what am i supposed to do
when you invent the words i use
to show what i'm going through
hiphop precede my emotional truth
it's the language that i bang with but if i aim it at you
we both left hanging
that's a dangerous tool
but my art is the part that's worth risking it for
so we stuck in an alley grippin our swords
an i hope that we both make it out on our own
but a hand'll be welcome if i can't stand alone
and i'm sorry for harming a hair on your head
i'm tryin be brave but i'm scared uh death
